name: Aspirin Synthesis
summary: Acetylation of salicylic acid with acetic anhydride to produce aspirin and acetic acid as a by-product.
solvent: Often solvent-light or acetic acid rich conditions
notes: Great MVP reaction because it has a clear transformation, familiar product and clean reaction storytelling.

steps.title: Charge Reactants
steps.description: Salicylic acid and acetic anhydride are combined to create an acetylation-ready mixture.

steps.title: Activate and Heat
steps.description: A catalytic acid and gentle heating accelerate acetyl transfer onto the phenolic oxygen.

steps.title: Form Product
steps.description: Aspirin forms while acetic acid appears as a by-product in the reaction matrix.

steps.title: Crystallize
steps.description: The mixture is cooled and diluted to isolate aspirin crystals for washing and drying.

name: Ethyl Acetate Esterification
summary: Fischer esterification of acetic acid and ethanol into the solvent ethyl acetate with water as coproduct.
notes: This storyboard represents acid-catalyzed equilibrium chemistry and solvent manufacturing logic.

steps.title: Mix Feedstocks
steps.description: Acetic acid and ethanol establish the equilibrium mixture before catalysis.

steps.title: Catalyze
steps.description: Acid catalysis activates the carbonyl and enables ester bond formation.

steps.title: Shift Equilibrium
steps.description: Removing water or using excess feed helps drive the equilibrium toward product.

name: Ethanol Combustion
summary: A process-side example showing ethanol oxidized to carbon dioxide and water under combustion conditions.
notes: Useful to visualize process chemistry, energy release and emission products even without full reaction engineering detail.

steps.title: Fuel Feed
steps.description: Ethanol enters a high-energy oxidative zone where bonds are rapidly redistributed.

steps.title: Oxidation
steps.description: Carbon is converted into carbon dioxide and hydrogen into water.
